test_name: No false alarm on clean update

artifact.content_preview:
From: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Site A AD Migration — Progress Update
Date: March 6, 2026

Alice,

Quick update on the Active Directory migration preparation for Site A:

1. Domain Controller Staging: The two new DC servers at Site A have been
   racked, powered on, and joined to the test domain. Windows Server
   patching is complete (all critical patches as of March 1 applied).

2. Schema Extension Testing: The AD schema extensio

extracted_tasks.text: Promote AD schema extension to staging
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Ready to promote to staging this weekend.
we need the Catalyst 9500s in place before we can do final network connectivity testing

extracted_tasks.text: Perform final network connectivity testing for DCs
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: final network connectivity testing for the DCs
23 are duplicates that can be deleted.

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Migrate 24 unique GPOs using reviewed script
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: 24 unique GPOs will be migrated. The migration script is ready

metadata.subject: Site A AD Migration — Progress Update

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments",
    "Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024",
    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024",
    "D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025",
    "D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025",
    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor",
    "Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "Compliance required with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance required with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance required with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance required with the Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables"
  ]
}
```

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor
Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
Compliance required with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
Compliance required with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
Compliance required with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
Compliance required with the Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.97,
  "drift_type": "within_scope",
  "detected_ask": "",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A; AD schema extension and forest restructuring; Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
  "suggested_action": "No change control action required. PM should acknowledge the update, log the March 18 migration window as a key internal milestone, and confirm Catalyst 9500 switch delivery timeline to ensure the noted dependency does not threaten the April 30, 2026 Go-Live (D-006) constraint.",
  "reasoning": "All five items reported in this email map directly to in-scope deliverables and activities: DC staging and domain join align with the AD domain controller consolidation at Site A; schema extension testing aligns with the AD schema extension and forest restructuring work item; GPO consolidation is a standard sub-task of AD migration (migrating 24 unique GPOs from Sites B and C to Site A); backup and rollback procedures are expected operational controls within the Migration Runbook (D-004); and the Catalyst 9500 switch dependency is explicitly in scope. No new requirements, budget asks, out-of-scope activities, or contradictions to the baseline are introduced. The email is a routine technical progress update. The only item warranting PM attention is the flagged dependency on Catalyst 9500 delivery — a schedule risk that should be tracked against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline but does not constitute scope drift."
}
```

parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A; AD schema extension and forest restructuring; Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
parsed_json.suggested_action: No change control action required. PM should acknowledge the update, log the March 18 migration window as a key internal milestone, and confirm Catalyst 9500 switch delivery timeline to ensure the noted dependency does not threaten the April 30, 2026 Go-Live (D-006) constraint.
parsed_json.reasoning: All five items reported in this email map directly to in-scope deliverables and activities: DC staging and domain join align with the AD domain controller consolidation at Site A; schema extension testing aligns with the AD schema extension and forest restructuring work item; GPO consolidation is a standard sub-task of AD migration (migrating 24 unique GPOs from Sites B and C to Site A); backup and rollback procedures are expected operational controls within the Migration Runbook (D-004); and the Catalyst 9500 switch dependency is explicitly in scope. No new requirements, budget asks, out-of-scope activities, or contradictions to the baseline are introduced. The email is a routine technical progress update. The only item warranting PM attention is the flagged dependency on Catalyst 9500 delivery — a schedule risk that should be tracked against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline but does not constitute scope drift.
